desc: All HTTP requests omit timeouts, so the program can hang indefinitely on a stalled server or connection.
The first request catches and only prints every Exception, hiding failures from callers and allowing execution to continue in a partially failed state.
The bare except catches BaseException subclasses such as KeyboardInterrupt and SystemExit and silently converts them into a print message.
The POST request is outside either try block, so network exceptions terminate the program despite the earlier requests being handled.
The first GET and POST never validate HTTP status codes, causing error responses to be printed as ordinary results.
The module-level requests.Session is never closed, leaving its connection-pool resources unmanaged for the process lifetime.
One tightly coupled function performs three unrelated HTTP operations and console reporting through globals, making reuse, testing, and independent error handling difficult.
